title: eriogonum kennedyi subsp. typicum
intro: Eriogonum kennedyi subsp. typicum, also known as the Apache Plume, is a type of flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The Apache Plume has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing respiratory issues, and aiding in the treatment of skin conditions and wounds. The plant is typically prepared for medicinal use through infusion, decoction, or tincture, and can be consumed as a tea or applied topically to the affected area.
